title: strobilanthes wightii
intro: Strobilanthes wightii, also known as the "Pongum Pulli," is a flowering plant species native to the Western Ghats of India and Sri Lanka. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, Strobilanthes wightii is often made into a decoction or a paste, which can be applied topically or consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
